trialDescriptions.title: Eine cluster-randomisiert kontrollierte Studie zur Evaluation eines schulbasierten Programms zur Prävention des Vapens und Rauchens bei Schulkindern in der 5. Jahrgangsstufe
trialDescriptions.summary: Die vorliegende Studie prüft die Effekte eines schulbasierten Vaping- und Rauchpräventionsprogramms auf die Motivation, das Rauchen und Vapen bei Schulkindern in der 5. Jahrgangsstufe. Kinder, die das Programm mit drei Workshops erhalten, werden mit Kindern verglichen, die den Standardunterricht an ihrer Schule erhalten. Alle Kinder werden 3, 6 und 12 Monate nach der ersten Basisbefragung erneut befragt. Anhand der Daten von der Befragung nach 12 Monaten wird bewertet, ob das Programm Kinder wirksam zu einem rauch- und vapefreien Leben motiviert.

trialDescriptions.title: A cluster randomized controlled trial of a school-based vaping and smoking prevention intervention aimed at schoolchildren in Grade 5
trialDescriptions.summary: This study examines the effects of a school-based vaping and smoking prevention program on motivation, smoking and vaping in school children in Year 5. Children receiving the three-workshop program will be compared to children receiving the standard curriculum at their school. All children are surveyed again 3, 6 and 12 months after the first survey. The data from the 12-month survey will be used to assess whether the program is effective in motivating children to live a smoke- and vape-free life.
trialDescriptions.scientificSummary:
The primary objective of this cluster-randomized controlled trial is to evaluate the effects of a school-based prevention program with three training sessions on motivation, smoking, and vaping (primary outcome: intention to be vape- and smoke-free across all modes of use, i.e., cigarettes, disposable e-cigarettes, reusable/refillable e-cigarettes, hookah, tobacco heater, cigars/ cigarillos, pipe) in children in Grade 5 (Age: 10–11 years) at 3, 6, and 12 months after baseline, compared to an active control group receiving the standard curriculum. 

We hypothesize that children in the intervention group, compared with children in the control group, will exhibit
1.	increased intentions to be vape- and smoke-free (primary endpoint measured at the 12-month follow-up) and 
2.	decreased susceptibility to vaping/smoking; vaping/smoking prevalence (lifetime prevalence, 12-month and 30-day prevalence, as well as 30-day-frequency), regular vaping/smoking (≥once a week), and current smoking/vaping (≥ once a week during the last month).

3.	The intervention effects are mediated via 
increased action planning for vaping/smoking refusal / saying “no” to vaping/smoking offers; increased vaping/smoking refusal skills (i.e., refusal self-concept and refusal self-efficacy as proxies for actual capabilities); increased self-efficacy to be vape- and smoke-free; increased positive attitudes towards being vape- and smoke-free, 
increased stress and problem coping (i.e., self-efficacy and planning for coping); and heightened intentions to be vape- and smoke-free (for the outcomes of susceptibility and vaping/smoking behavior only); decreased perceived vaping/smoking norms; decreased negative attitudes towards being vape- and smoke-free.

4.	We will further test hypotheses about differences in risk and protective factors, as well as in vaping/smoking outcomes, by social determinants of health (gender, SES, religion/world view, migration history, spoken language, and health status). A detailed description of this examination can be found in our pre-registration file: https://osf.io/sftvp?view_only=bdee3018d9e245fb96dc6fa06be42ee2.  Additionally, we will explore differential intervention effectiveness as well as underlying mechanisms of change by subgroup.

observationalGroups.comment:
Interventionsgruppe: Drei interaktive Workshops im Schulunterricht im Abstand von jeweils 3 Monaten, geleitet durch Personen aus dem Studienteam mit einem Hintergrund in Psychologie, Humanmedizin oder Gesundheitswissenschaften. 
Workshop 1: 180 Minuten, alternativ in zwei separaten 90-Minuten-Einheiten aufgeteilt: Themen: Gesundheitliche Konsequenzen des Rauchens und Vapens, soziale Einflüsse und kritisches Denken 
Workshop 2: 90 Minuten, davon 45 Minuten unterrichtet durch die Klassenlehrkraft, 35 Minuten durch das Studienteam
Workshop 3: 90 Minuten Lebenskompetenztraining mit Fokus auf den Umgang mit unangenehmen Emotionen und Problemlösung

Intervention group: Three interactive workshops in the classroom, each 3 months apart, led by members of the study team with a background in psychology, human medicine or health sciences. 
Workshop 1: 180 minutes, alternatively split into two separate 90-minute sessions: Topics: Health consequences of smoking and vaping, social influences and critical thinking 
Workshop 2: 90 minutes, 45 minutes taught by the class teacher, 35 minutes by the study team
Workshop 3: 90 minutes of life skills training with a focus on dealing with unpleasant emotions and problem solving

Aktive Kontrollgruppe: Erhält das Standardcurriculum durch Lehrkräfte. Dieses soll laut Rahmenlehrplan die Sucht- und Tabakprävention beinhalten. 

Landesinstitut für Schule und Medien Berlin-Brandenburg. Bildungsserver Berlin-Brandenburg: Rahmenlehrplan Online: Nawi 5/6 [Framework Curriculum Online: Natural Sciences 5/6]. Bildungsserver Berlin -Brandenburg. 2024. https://bildungsserver.berlin-brandenburg.de/rlp-online/c-faecher/nawi-56/themen-und-inhalte. Accessed 23 Apr 2024.

Active control: Standard curriculum taught by class teachers, which is meant to cover the prevention of addiction and tobacco use. 

Landesinstitut für Schule und Medien Berlin-Brandenburg. Bildungsserver Berlin-Brandenburg: Rahmenlehrplan Online: Nawi 5/6 [Framework Curriculum Online: Natural Sciences 5/6]. Bildungsserver Berlin -Brandenburg. 2024. https://bildungsserver.berlin-brandenburg.de/rlp-online/c-faecher/nawi-56/themen-und-inhalte. Accessed 23 Apr 2024.

characteristicDescriptions.primaryOutcome:
Der primäre Endpunkt dieser Studie ist der Unterschied hinsichtlich Intention der Schüler, vape- und rauchfrei zu sein (d. h. auf den Konsum von Vaping- oder Tabakprodukten zu verzichten) zwischen der Interventions- und der Kontrollgruppe (primärer Endpunkt, gemessen bei T4, 12 Monate nach der Intervention), gemessen anhand von zwei Items, die jeweils eine 5-Punkte-Likert-Skala verwenden, d. h.: „Ich möchte in den nächsten sechs Monaten rauchfrei sein (d. h. nicht rauchen)“ (1 = stimme überhaupt nicht zu, 5 = stimme voll und ganz zu) (Ajzen, 2009). 

Bevor die Schüler befragt werden, macht das Studienteam sie mit der befragungsspezifischen Terminologie vertraut (d. h. „Rauchen“ oder „rauchfrei sein“ als Oberbegriffe für den Konsum von/ die Abstinenz von Tabak- oder Vaping-Produkten). 

Ajzen MF Icek. Predicting and changing behavior: The reasoned action approach. New York: Psychology Press; 2009.
characteristicDescriptions.secondaryOutcome:
Zu den sekundären Ergebnissen gehören:
Anfälligkeit für das Vapen oder Rauchen (Pierce et. al, 1996; Choi et. al, 2001; Stroong et. al, 2015): binär kodiert (0 = festgelegte Nicht-Vaper und Nicht-Raucher, 1 = anfällig für Vapen/Rauchen). Die Schüler werden gebeten, ihr erwartetes Verhalten in drei Szenarien anzugeben, z. B.: „Glauben Sie, dass Sie in Zukunft versuchen werden zu rauchen?“, wobei eine 4-Punkte-Likert-Skala verwendet wird (1 = definitiv nicht, 4 = definitiv). Schüler werden nur dann als überzeugte/festgelegte Nichtraucher und Nichtraucher (bzw. als für das Vapen/Rauchen anfällig) betrachtet, wenn sie alle Fragen mit „definitiv ja“ beantwortet haben.  

Prävalenz des Vapens/Rauchens: Lebenszeitprävalenz, 12-Monats-Prävalenz, 30-Tage-Prävalenz, binäre Codierung)

Frequenz des Vapens/Rauchens: 30-Tage-Frequenz; aktuelles Rauchen (binär definiert als Angabe eines aktuellen Konsums, einschließlich eines nur gelegentlichen Konsums im letzten Monat);  regelmäßiges Vapen/Rauchen (binär definiert als Vapen oder Rauchen mehr als einmal pro Woche im letzten Monat.

Frequenz und Prävalenzmaße werden anhand von angepassten Maßen aus dem KiGGS, Welle 1; Kuntz et. al, 2015; Lampert et. al, 2008, erhoben. Sie werden jeweils getrennt für einzelne Produktkategorien (d. h. Zigaretten, Einweg-E-Zigaretten, wiederverwendbare/nachfüllbare E-Zigaretten, Wasserpfeife, Tabakerhitzer, Zigarren/Zigarillos, Pfeife)erhoben  und dann über alle Produkte aggregiert, um die Prävalenz/Häufigkeit über alle Produkte für die Hauptanalyse zu berechnen. 

Pierce JP, Choi WS, Gilpin EA, Farkas AJ, Merritt RK. Validation of susceptibility as a predictor of which adolescents take up smoking in the United States. Health Psychology. 1996;15:355–61.

Choi WS, Gilpin EA, Farkas AJ, Pierce JP. Determining the probability of future smoking among adolescents. Addiction. 2001;96:313–23.

Strong DR, Hartman SJ, Nodora J, Messer K, James L, White M, et al. Predictive Validity of the Expanded Susceptibility to Smoke Index. Nicotine & Tobacco Research. 2015;17:862–9.

Kuntz B, Lampert T, KiGGS Study Group. Wasserpfeifenkonsum (Shisha-Rauchen) bei Jugendlichen in Deutschland. Bundesgesundheitsbl. 2015;58:467–73.

Lampert T. Smoking and Passive Smoking Exposure in Young People – Results of the German Health Interview and Examination Survey for Children and Adolescents (KiGGS). Deutsches Ärzteblatt international. 2008. https://doi.org/10.3238/arztebl.2008.0265.
characteristicDescriptions.typeOfHiddenAllocation: Randomization will be carried out at school level to avoid possible spill-over effects of the prevention intervention.  On invitation to the study and briefing of headmasters by the study coordinator, schools will not have have been allocated to the intervention or control groups yet. Once the headmaster has signed consent to participate, the study coordinator conducts the random allocation using R.
characteristicDescriptions.typeOfSequenceGeneration:
Schools will be stratified according to 2 factors: The Berlin School Classification (“Schultypisierungsindex (STYPS)”, provided by the Berlin Senate Department For Education, Youth and Family, Department of Education Statistics, which reflects the level of stress in Berlin schools and thus provides an indication of the socio-structural burden of the students at the school level, and the number of participating classes in order to achieve a balanced number of students in the two groups. Using the same factors,  four different randomization groups will be created: schools with higher structural strain and low number of classes, schools with lower structural strain and high number of classes, schools with higher structural strain and high number of classes and schools with higher structural strain and low number of classes. A score of 1-3 on the STYPS will be defined as an indicator of lower structural distress, while 4-7 indicated higher distress for the schools. A high number of classes was defined as 4 or more classes.

The blocks of schools (varying in size) will be allocated sequentially, as the  recruitment phase continues during randomization. Randomization will be done from a minimum of 2 schools per block.

Random allocation of the schools to the intervention or control groups will be done using R in a 1:1 ratio by the study coordinator who has recruited the schools, but will not be involved in data analysis. A computer-generated list of random seeds, which are determined prior to allocation, will be used for the automatic randomization into groups.

characteristicDescriptions.primaryOutcome:
The primary outcome for this study is the difference in students’ intention to be vape- and smoke-free (i.e., to abstain from using any vaping or tobacco product) between intervention and control groups (primary endpoint measured at T4, 12 months post-intervention), measured by two items, each of them using a 5-Point-Likert-Scale, i. e.: “I want to be smoke-free (that is, not to smoke) for the next six months” (1 = strongly disagree, 5 = fully agree) (Ajzen, 2009). 

Before students are being surveyed, the instructors familiarize them with the survey-specific terminology (i.e., “smoking” or “being smoke-free” as umbrella terms for use of/abstinence from any tobacco or vaping product).

Ajzen MF Icek. Predicting and changing behavior: The reasoned action approach. New York: Psychology Press; 2009.
characteristicDescriptions.secondaryOutcome:
Secondary outcomes include:
Susceptibility to vaping or smoking: Assessed based on items used in previous research  (Pierce et. al, 1996; Choi et. al, 2001; Stroong et. al, 2015) and coded binarily (0 = Committed non-vapers and non-smokers, 1 = Susceptible to vape/smoke). Students will be asked to report their expected behavior in three scenarios, i.e.: “Do you think you will try smoking in the future?”, using a 4-Point-Likert scale (1 = definitely not, 4 = definitely). Students will be considered committed non-vapers and non-smokers (vs. susceptible to vaping/smoking) only if they report “definitely yes” on all questions.  

Vaping/smoking prevalence (Lifetime prevalence, 12-months prevalence, 30-day prevalence, binary coding) and
Vaping/smoking frequency (30-day-frequency; current smoking, binarily defined as reporting any current consumption, including only occasional use during the last month; regular vaping/smoking, binarily defined as vaping or smoking more than once a week during the last month): Assessed using adjusted measures from the KiGGS (German Health Interview and Examination Survey for Children and Adolescents, Wave 1; Kuntz et. al, 2015; Lampert et. al, 2008); assessed separately for individual product categories (i.e., cigarettes, disposable e-cigarettes, reusable/refillable e-cigarettes, hookah, tobacco heater, cigars/ cigarillos, pipe); aggregated to calculate prevalence/frequencies across products for the main analysis. 

Pierce JP, Choi WS, Gilpin EA, Farkas AJ, Merritt RK. Validation of susceptibility as a predictor of which adolescents take up smoking in the United States. Health Psychology. 1996;15:355–61.

Choi WS, Gilpin EA, Farkas AJ, Pierce JP. Determining the probability of future smoking among adolescents. Addiction. 2001;96:313–23.

Strong DR, Hartman SJ, Nodora J, Messer K, James L, White M, et al. Predictive Validity of the Expanded Susceptibility to Smoke Index. Nicotine & Tobacco Research. 2015;17:862–9.

Kuntz B, Lampert T, KiGGS Study Group. Wasserpfeifenkonsum (Shisha-Rauchen) bei Jugendlichen in Deutschland. Bundesgesundheitsbl. 2015;58:467–73.

Lampert T. Smoking and Passive Smoking Exposure in Young People – Results of the German Health Interview and Examination Survey for Children and Adolescents (KiGGS). Deutsches Ärzteblatt international. 2008. https://doi.org/10.3238/arztebl.2008.0265.

trialResultsDescriptions.ipdSharingPlanDescription:
We will share individual pariticipant data that underlie any of our published results, after identification. 
We will publish the study protocol, statistical analysis plan, analytic code, informed consent forms. 

The data will be made available beginning 6 months following article publication; no end date. 
The deidentified data will be made indefinetly available via OSF: https://osf.io/sftvp?view_only=bdee3018d9e245fb96dc6fa06be42ee2, to anyone who wishes to access it; for analyses to achieve the described aims of the study.

studiedHealthConditionDescriptions.comment: Psychische und Verhaltensstörungen durch Tabak: Abhängigkeitssyndrom
Mental and behavioural disorders due to use of tobacco: Dependence syndrome

recruitmentDescriptions.exclusionCriteria: Die Schulen müssen sich schriftlich verpflichten, keine anderen externen Anbieter für die Raucherprävention in der Zielgruppe der Fünftklässler zu engagieren.  Sollten die Schulen diese Zustimmung verweigern, müssen sie sich zumindest bereiterklären, dem Studienteam alle externen Anbieter mitzuteilen, die an der Raucherpräventionsarbeit mit den Klassen beteiligt sind.  Schulen, die nicht bereit sind, einer dieser beiden Bedingungen zuzustimmen (Verzicht auf Einbindung oder Mitteilung darüber), werden nicht in die Studie aufgenommen.
recruitmentDescriptions.inclusionCriteria:
Schulen in den Berliner Bezirken Neukölln, Mitte, Reinickendorf, Lichtenberg, Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg und Spandau werden in die Studie einbezogen. Zu den teilnahmeberechtigten Schulen gehören Grundschulen und Gesamtschulen mit den Klassenstufen 5 und 6. 
Klassen der Jahrgangsstufe 5 sind teilnahmeberechtigt (daher kann das Alter der einzelnen Schüler variieren). 
Schulen mit jahrgangsgemischten Klassen (d. h. Klassen, die üblicherweise in altersgemischten Gruppen unterrichtet werden) werden in die Studie aufgenommen, sofern für den Unterricht des Präventionsprogramms neue Klassengruppen gebildet werden können (nur Fünft- bzw. Sechstklässler). Um einzelne Schüler einer teilnehmenden Schulklasse in die Studie einzubeziehen, ist die schriftliche Einwilligung sowohl der Schüler als auch ihrer Eltern zur Datenerhebung erforderlich.

recruitmentDescriptions.exclusionCriteria: Schools are required to provide written consent not to engage other external providers for smoking prevention among the target group of 5th graders.  If schools should refuse to consent to it, they must at least agree to notify the study team of any external providers involved in smoking prevention work with the classes.  Schools that are unwilling to agree to either of those conditions (abstain or notify) will not be included in the study.
recruitmentDescriptions.inclusionCriteria:
Schools in the Berlin districts of Neukölln, Mitte, Reinickendorf, Lichtenberg, Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg and Spandau will be included in the study. The eligible schools included elementary school and comprehensive schools with grades 5 and 6. 
Grade 5 classes will be eligible to be enrolled (thus, age of individual students may vary). 
Schools with mixed-grade classes (i.e., classes that are usually being taught in age-mixed groups) will be included in the study provided that new class groups will be formed for the teaching of the prevention program (only 5th and 6th graders). In order to include individual students from a participating school class in the study, informed written consent for data collection will be required from both the students and their parents.
